title: Bryophyllum × houghtonii
intro: Bryophyllum × houghtonii, also known as the Kalanchoe or Life Plant, is a hybrid succulent species widely cultivated for its ornamental value and medicinal properties. The health benefits of Bryophyllum × houghtonii include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in reducing swelling and fighting free radical damage in the body. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, as well as glycosides and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Bryophyllum × houghtonii is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Bryophyllum × houghtonii is a hybrid species of the genus Bryophyllum, which is part of the family Crassulaceae. This classification places it within the order Saxifragales and the clade rosids. The family Crassulaceae is further classified under the larger clade rosids, which also includes other flowering plant families such as the Cistaceae and the Iteaceae. Bryophyllum × houghtonii is a type of succulent plant, characterized by its thick, fleshy leaves. The exact classification of Bryophyllum × houghtonii may vary depending on the source or specific botanical system used.
uses: Bryophyllum × houghtonii, also known as the Kalanchoe houghtonii, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
